executiveSummary.text: Risk-off shock: S&P 500 -2.64%, Nasdaq -4.18%, Russell 2000 -3.47%, VIX 21.5 (+40%). Strong US jobs / rate-hike repricing hit duration and crowded AI beta together.
Rates/USD tightened conditions: 10Y Treasury 4.54% (+5.9bp by Yahoo proxy), 30Y near 5.00%, DXY 100.07 (+0.66%). This is a discount-rate selloff, not yet credit/liquidity panic.
AI/semis suffered a positioning reset after Broadcom did not raise 2026 AI sales guidance: NVDA -6.2%, TSM -6.7%, ASML -6.6%, MU -13.3%, LRCX/KLAC/AMAT about -9–10%, AVGO -7.9%, SNPS -6.0%.

Scarcity assets did not hedge today: gold -2.7%, silver -7.8%, copper -3.6%, oil -2–3%, COPX -10.6%, URNM -9.5%. USD/real-rate pressure and forced de-risking overwhelmed inflation/geopolitical narratives.
Singapore ballast held: DBS -0.6%, OCBC -0.25%, UOB +0.6%, REITs roughly flat to -1.2%. KL's barbell is working as designed: cash/CPF/property and SG dividend sleeve dampen the US AI drawdown.

regime.label: Risk-off / higher-real-rate reset, not yet systemic stress

usGlobal.what: US equities sold off sharply with tech leadership breaking hardest.
usGlobal.why: Fresh strong-labor-market reporting pushed investors toward higher-for-longer or even hike-risk pricing; high-duration AI winners were most vulnerable.
usGlobal.implication: Base case is valuation compression until yields stabilize; breadth matters more than index level next.

usGlobal.what: VIX jumped above 21 but is not crisis-level.
usGlobal.why: Options demand rose after a crowded trade unwound; no evidence here of funding stress comparable with a systemic event.
usGlobal.implication: Do not panic-sell, but wait for realized-volatility compression before adding aggressively.

ratesMacro.why: Growth/labor resilience reduces near-term Fed-cut optionality and lifts real discount rates.
ratesMacro.implication: Long-duration equities, REITs and gold face pressure; cash at ~4% remains valuable dry powder.

ratesMacro.what: Probability framework: 55% choppy higher-for-longer consolidation, 25% deeper 8–12% equity correction, 15% quick AI-led rebound, 5% systemic liquidity stress.
ratesMacro.why: Macro shock is material but VIX/credit signals are not yet extreme; confidence medium.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore large caps were stable relative to US tech; banks mixed and REITs mildly soft.
asiaSingapore.why: SG banks benefit from higher-for-longer rates up to a point; REIT cap rates remain rate-sensitive but price action was controlled.
asiaSingapore.implication: SG dividend sleeve continues to act as portfolio ballast.

asiaSingapore.why: Global dollar bid followed higher US yields.
asiaSingapore.implication: USD assets get FX cushion in SGD terms, partially offsetting equity losses for KL.

commodities.what: Gold, silver, copper and oil all fell despite geopolitical headlines.
commodities.why: Higher real yields + stronger USD + de-risking liquidation dominated scarcity/inflation narratives.
commodities.implication: Scarcity thesis intact structurally, but tactically these assets can behave like risk assets when liquidity tightens.

commodities.what: Copper and uranium equities were hit harder than underlying commodities.
commodities.why: Mining ETFs embed operating leverage and equity beta; when discount rates rise, future resource cash flows get marked down.
commodities.implication: Rebalance only by pre-set tranches; do not assume commodities always hedge tech selloffs.

geopolitics.what: Middle East / shipping / China oil demand headlines remain relevant but did not drive today's prices.
geopolitics.why: Oil fell with risk assets, implying demand/liquidity concerns outweighed supply-risk premium in the last session.
geopolitics.implication: Transmission path to watch: shipping insurance/freight → oil/products → inflation expectations → Fed path.

moves.what: AI/Semicaps sleeve abnormal drawdown: MU -13.3%, LRCX -9.8%, AMAT -9.7%, KLAC -9.5%, AVGO -7.9%, TSM -6.7%, ASML -6.6%, NVDA -6.2%, SNPS -6.0%.
moves.why: Broadcom earnings/news did not lift 2026 AI sales guidance despite strong AI demand, causing crowded AI-capex names to reprice as rates rose.
moves.implication: Thesis damage low-to-moderate: this questions valuation/expectations more than the need for compute, HBM, foundry, EDA and equipment. 2nd order: hyperscalers may gain relative safety; 3rd order: if financing costs stay high, marginal AI capex gets scrutinized.
moves.action: HOLD / staged ADD only after 2–3 sessions of stabilization or if positions hit pre-defined allocation bands; invalidator: hyperscaler capex cuts or order cancellations, not price weakness alone.

moves.what: Commodities sleeve fell sharply: COPX -10.6%, SLV -8.1%, GLD -3.7%, URNM -9.5%; copper -3.6%, silver -7.8%, gold -2.7%.
moves.why: Dollar and rate shock plus equity beta liquidation; miners amplified the move.
moves.implication: Structural scarcity thesis remains, but portfolio correlation rose in stress. Avoid increasing all scarcity assets at once; copper/uranium entries should be tranche-based.
moves.action: WATCH / ADD in small tranches only if copper holds key support and DXY/yields stop rising; invalidator: broad demand recession or China demand deterioration.

moves.what: GRID -4.8% and QQQ -4.8% breached ETF abnormal-move thresholds.
moves.why: Duration-sensitive growth infrastructure sold with tech; grid/power theme is still AI-linked but not immune to discount-rate shocks.
moves.implication: Power bottleneck thesis intact, but near-term multiple compression can continue if 10Y holds above ~4.5%.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only on yield stabilization or a further 5–8% drawdown with no thesis deterioration.

moves.what: SG dividend/bank sleeve remained calm: DBS -0.6%, OCBC -0.25%, UOB +0.6%, Keppel -0.7%, Singtel -0.7%, Ascendas REIT -1.2%.
moves.why: Local income assets were less exposed to US AI-duration unwind; higher rates support bank NIMs but cap REIT multiples.
moves.implication: This is the ballast KL pays for via lower upside; it reduces need to sell growth assets into weakness.
moves.action: HOLD; consider REIT adds only if 10Y yield rolls over or spreads become unusually wide.

thesisImpact.why: AI supply-chain and commodity assets fell together under real-rate/liquidity pressure.
thesisImpact.implication: The thesis is not broken, but its risk model must assume positive correlation during rate shocks.
thesisImpact.action: Keep barbell; use cash, not forced selling, as the shock absorber.

thesisImpact.what: NVDA underweight helped relative pain control
thesisImpact.why: NVDA fell -6.2%, but KL's AI exposure is spread across foundry/equipment/memory/EDA/hyperscalers.
thesisImpact.implication: Diversification within AI supply chain matters; do not chase single-name hero trades after a volatility spike.
thesisImpact.action: IGNORE short-term NVDA regret/FOMO; revisit only at valuation or thesis trigger.

narratives.title: AI capex reality check, not AI winter
narratives.description: Market is repricing the gap between excellent AI demand and extremely high embedded expectations. The important signal is whether hyperscaler capex plans/order books weaken; today's evidence is mainly valuation and positioning pressure.
narratives.source: Yahoo Finance/CNBC/Brave results within 24h; market prices via Yahoo Finance
narratives.priceImpact: negative short-term; opportunity if fundamentals confirm

narratives.title: Higher-for-longer becomes higher-again risk
narratives.description: Strong employment data revived concerns that the Fed cannot ease quickly. Second order: real yields rise; third order: equity risk premium and private-market funding stress become more important.

narratives.title: Commodities fail as hedge in liquidity shock
narratives.description: Gold/oil/metals sold together because the channel was USD and real yields, not pure supply fear. This is exactly why KL's cash ballast is distinct from commodity exposure.
narratives.source: Kitco/Trading Economics/OilPrice snippets and futures prices

secondOrderEffects.text: 1st order: strong jobs/rate-hike bets raise yields. 2nd order: duration equities and gold fall as real discount rates rise. 3rd order: if higher yields persist, corporate capex hurdle rates rise and marginal AI infrastructure projects face more scrutiny.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Watch hyperscaler capex commentary, credit spreads and 10Y real yields before adding aggressively.

secondOrderEffects.text: 1st order: Broadcom disappoints elevated expectations. 2nd order: entire AI supply chain de-rates. 3rd order: relative winners may be cash-rich hyperscalers and bottleneck suppliers with pricing power, while highly levered or second-tier AI beneficiaries lag.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Prefer quality bottlenecks over speculative AI adjacency.

secondOrderEffects.text: 1st order: USD strengthens. 2nd order: commodities and EM/Asia FX face pressure. 3rd order: Singapore USD-denominated assets get SGD translation cushion while local REITs face cap-rate pressure.
secondOrderEffects.implication: SGD-based portfolio loss is damped, but REIT adds need yield confirmation.

investment.thesis: Maintain AI scarcity core but upgrade quality bias: foundry/equipment/EDA/HBM plus cash-rich hyperscalers.
investment.trigger: Add only in 3–4 tranches if QQQ/semis stabilize for 2–3 sessions or if individual names fall another 5–10% without capex/order deterioration.
investment.whyNow: The selloff is valuation/positioning-led; long-term compute bottlenecks remain plausible.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, HBM inventory glut, or foundry/equipment order cancellations.

investment.thesis: Keep SG banks/dividend sleeve as ballast; it is doing its job.
investment.trigger: Rebalance only if SG sleeve becomes materially underweight or dividend yields become unusually attractive versus cash.
investment.whyNow: US tech volatility shows the value of non-correlated income assets.
investment.invalidator: Singapore credit cycle deterioration or sharp NIM compression.

trading.thesis: Do not catch falling knives in semis on day one; wait for volatility confirmation.
trading.trigger: VIX below 20 and SMH/QQQ positive reversal day on above-average volume, or 10Y back below ~4.45%.
trading.whyNow: Crowded unwind can overshoot for several sessions.

trading.thesis: Gold/miners are not automatic crisis hedges when USD/real yields rise.
trading.trigger: Consider GLD/SLV/COPX only when DXY stalls and real yields stop rising.
trading.whyNow: Today's -3% to -10% moves may tempt premature averaging down.

watchlist.thesis: Credit/liquidity confirmation is the line between correction and systemic risk.
watchlist.trigger: HY spreads jump, VIX >25–30, funding stress headlines, or private-credit redemption gates widen.
watchlist.whyNow: Search results flagged private funding stress; equity volatility alone is not enough.
watchlist.invalidator: Spreads stable and VIX fades below 18.

watchlist.thesis: AI capex durability is the key portfolio thesis variable.
watchlist.trigger: Next hyperscaler/Broadcom/Nvidia/TSMC commentary on orders, lead times, HBM supply and data-center power constraints.
watchlist.whyNow: Price action is challenging expectations; fundamentals must now confirm.
watchlist.invalidator: Broad capex cuts or falling utilization.

riskWatch.name: Rates shock / Fed repricing
riskWatch.description: 10Y around 4.54%, 30Y near 5%; higher real rates pressure long-duration equities, REITs and gold.
riskWatch.mitigation: Stage buys; keep cash buffer; avoid margin/leverage.

riskWatch.name: Crowded AI unwind
riskWatch.description: Semis and AI infrastructure ETFs breached abnormal-move thresholds across KL holdings.
riskWatch.mitigation: Separate thesis from valuation; add only after stabilization and no order/capex deterioration.

riskWatch.name: Liquidity/credit stress
riskWatch.description: VIX spike is notable but not crisis-level; private-credit/redemption headlines bear monitoring.
riskWatch.mitigation: Track HYG/LQD spreads, funding headlines, bank stress; preserve dry powder.

riskWatch.name: Commodity correlation failure
riskWatch.description: Gold, copper, uranium and oil fell together under USD/real-rate pressure.
riskWatch.mitigation: Do not rely on commodities as sole hedge; cash/CPF/property ballast remains key.

keyEvents.text: US jobs strength revived higher-for-longer / hike-risk narrative; Nasdaq fell over 4%.
Broadcom-related AI expectation reset spread across semiconductors; Yahoo reported chip stocks shed more than $1T in value.
DXY rose to ~100.07 and Treasury yields climbed; commodities sold off instead of hedging.
keyEvents.source: Yahoo Finance market data / Brave search

keyEvents.text: Singapore banks and REITs were comparatively stable versus US tech drawdown.

bottomLine.whatMatters: This is a broad higher-real-rate / crowded-growth reset: US equities, AI semis, commodities and miners all sold off together while Singapore ballast held.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Stay with the barbell. Hold core AI/scarcity positions, add only by tranche after yields/VIX stabilize, and use cash as optionality rather than emotional comfort only.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore single-day P/L panic and simplistic 'gold/geopolitics hedge' narratives; the active transmission channel is real rates, USD, positioning and liquidity.
